attributes.desc: OIC - Summer Half Day Camp July 6-10
attributes.best_description:
SUMMER HALF DAY CAMPS 2026

Beginner 1 to Intermediate 3 Learn to Skate Skill Levels

(Ages 6 and up)

Our camps provide children at the Beginning and Intermediate levels the opportunity to skate,

play and grow in a positive, fun, and safe atmosphere.

  • All Skating Class students should wear warm clothing, gloves, long socks and clothes that allow them to move easily on the ice.
  • Please be patient with the staff and the skaters on the first day. We are doing our best to ensure that everyone in the camp is safe, happy and having fun.
  • Please do not block the doors to the ice. It impedes the flow of traffic for the skaters and coaches entering and exiting AND can be quite dangerous too. The doors should be closed at all times.
  • Please do not interrupt the instructors while they are working.
  • No Parents/Caregivers are allowed on the party room. Thank you.
  • Spectators can view from the bleachers upstairs.

Daily Day Camp Schedule:

(All activities are supervised by Oakland Ice Center Instructors)

  • 10:00 - AM - Drop Off and Skater Check In (Earlier drop off is not available)
  • 10:15 - Off Ice Warm Up (Running shoes please) then Skates On
  • 10:30 - On Ice with lesson
  • 11:15 - Snack (bring your own snack/water or purchase at our snack bar)
  • 11:30 - Free skate/practice with supervision
  • 12:30 - Exit the ice – skates off – Off Ice Warm Down
  • 1:00 PM - Pick Up Your Camper - On time please! Late pick ups will result in an extra fee.

NO REFUNDS OR EXCHANGES

NOTE: Please read the description for your registered class time.Class Time: 5:15pm - 5:45pmPractice/Free time: 5:45pm - 6:15pm Class curriculum: Tiny Tot 1: Proper way to fall and get-up. Marching while moving Two-foot jump in place.  There are no refunds, make ups or credits for missed classes.

Note:
Class Time: 5:15pm - 5:45pm
Practice/Free time: 5:45pm - 6:15pm

Tiny Tot 2:

  1. Beginner Two-Foot Glide
  2. Push and Glide Stroking
  3. Preparation for Snowplow Stop
  4. Dip
  5. Forward Swizzle


There are no refunds, make ups or credits for missed classes.

Note:
Class Time: 5:15pm - 5:45pm
Practice/Free time: 5:45pm - 6:15pm

Tiny Tot 3:

  1. T-Position and Push off Right & Left
  2. Backward Wiggles
  3. Two or One Foot Snowplow Stop
  4. Backward Swizzle



There are no refunds, make ups or credits for missed classes.

Beginner 1/Pre-Alpha First beginner level skating class for both Skating & Hockey ages 6-17 Class curriculum: Two-foot glide. One-foot glide/ right foot/ left foot. Forward Swizzle. Backward Wiggle. Backward Swizzle. Forward Pumps.There are no refunds, make ups or credits for missed classes.

Class Time: 5:15pm - 5:45pmPractice/Free time: 5:45pm - 6:15pm Prerequisite: Beginner 1/Pre-Alpha ClassClass curriculum: Forward Stroking. Forward Crossovers left over right. Forward Crossovers right over left. One foot snowplow stops. Two-foot turn There are no refunds, make ups or credits for missed classes.

Class Time: 5:15pm - 5:45pmPractice/Free time: 5:45pm - 6:15pm Beginner 3/Beta Third beginning level skating class for ages 6-17Prerequisite : Beginner 2/AlphaClass Curriculum: Backward Crossovers right over left. Backward Crossovers left over right. Backward Stroking. Right T-Stop. Left T-Stop. There are no refunds, make ups or credits for missed classes.

Private Hockey Lessons

Players must be on the ice with a Sharks Ice private coach!

Please note: This class shares ice with the youth learn to skate session at the same time. The first 30 minute are generally a practice/warm-up while the second 30 minutes are the class time. First week of classes will begin from 5:45pm without the practice/warm-up. Group instruction includes a 30 minute class and 30 minute supervised practice. Beginner 1 (Pre-Alpha equivalent) & Adult Beginner 1 Curriculum: 1. Two foot glide 2. One-foot glide/ right foot/ left foot 3. Forward Swizzle 4. Backward Wiggle 5. Backward Swizzle 6. Forward Pumps

Please note: This class shares ice with the youth learn to skate session at the same time. The first 30 minute are generally a practice/warm-up while the second 30 minutes are the class time. First week of classes will begin from 5:45pm without the practice/warm-up. Beginner 2 (Alpha equivalent) & Adult Beginner 2 Curriculum: 1. Forward Stroking 2. Forward Crossovers left over right (5) 3. Forward Crossovers right over left (5) 4. One foot snowplow stop 5. Two foot turn. Beginner 3 (Beta equivalent) & Adult Beginner 3 Curriculum: 1. Backward Crossovers right over left (5) 2. Backward Crossovers left over right (5) 3. Backward Stroking (6) 4. Right T-Stop 5. Left T-Stop 6. Introduction to Forward Edges

NOTE: Please read the description for your registered class time.
Practice/Warmup Time: 5:45pm - 6:15pm
Class time: 6:15pm - 6:45pm

Intermediate 1/Gamma First Intermediate level skating class for ages 6-17

Class Curriculum:

  1. Left Forward Outside 3-Turn
  2. Right Forward Outside 3-Turn
  3. Left Forward Inside Mohawk
  4. Right Forward Inside Mohawk
  5. Hockey Stop
  6. Introduction to Backward Edges

There is No refunds, make ups or credits for missed classes.

NOTE: Please read the description for your registered class time.
Practice/Warmup Time: 5:45pm - 6:15pm
Class time: 6:15pm - 6:45pm

Intermediate 2/Delta Second Intermediate level skating class for ages 6-17

Class Curriculum:

  1. Left Forward Inside 3-Turn
  2. Right Forward Inside 3-Turn
  3. Forward Outside Edges
  4. Forward Inside Edges
  5. Lunge or Shoot the Duck
  6. One-half Flip

There is No refunds, make ups or credits for missed classes.

Intermediate 1 - 3's curriculum introduces different forward to backward turns, edges, first jumps, and first spins. Must have completed Intermediate 2 (Delta).

Intermediate 3/Freestyle 1 Third Intermediate level skating class for ages 6-17

Class Curriculum:

  1. Backward outside edges
  2. Backward inside edges
  3. Two-foot spin
  4. Forward spiral
  5. Forward inside pivot
  6. Waltz jump

There is No refunds, make ups or credits for missed classes.

OIC - Gretzky Hour

Full Hockey Gear Required
Each player is required to check in at the front desk before each session and must wear their sticker receipt visible on their pants.
Full USA Hockey Certified equipment is required.
Limit 40 skaters and 4 goalies.
No outside or private coaches are allowed on the ice at any time. - Unless approved through management.

Ice Dance 1

(Formerly Introduction to Ice Dance)

30 minute class followed by 90 minute Ice Dance session

Open to children, youth and adults

 

Requirements:

Advanced level in the Learn To Skate program; Stroking, forward and backward cross overs, all edges, outside three turns and inside C-turns (Mohawks).

 

Instructors will introduce Ice Dance elements: swing rolls, progressives and chasses. Teach how to read dance diagrams for steps, patterns and timing. Teach the preliminary dances, introduce ice dance timing and expression (ex: tango vs waltz). Teach how to safely participate in Ice Dance practice and training sessions.

Ice Dance 2:

30 minute class followed by 90 minute Ice Dance session

Open to children, youth and adults

 

Prerequisite is Ice Dance 1 or USFS tests at the preliminary dance level

 

Instructors will continue per Ice Dance 1, refining and strengthening all elements teach the pre-bronze dances and per instructors’ discretion, the bronze dances teach safety and etiquette for Ice Dance Social Sessions.

Ice Dance 1

(Formerly Introduction to Ice Dance)

30 minute class followed by 90 minute Ice Dance session

Open to children, youth and adults

 

Requirements:

Advanced level in the Learn To Skate program; Stroking, forward and

backward cross overs, all edges, outside three turns and inside C-turns (Mohawks)

 

Instructors will introduce Ice Dance elements: swing rolls, progressives and chasses. Teach how to read dance diagrams for steps, patterns and timing. Teach the preliminary dances, introduce ice dance timing and expression (ex: tango vs waltz). Teach how to safely participate in Ice Dance practice and training sessions

Ice Dance 2:

30 minute class followed by 90 minute Ice Dance session

Open to children, youth and adults

 

Prerequisite is Ice Dance 1 or USFS tests at the preliminary dance level

 

Instructors will continue per Ice Dance 1, refining and strengthening all elements teach the pre-bronze dances and per instructors’ discretion, the bronze

dances teach safety and etiquette for Ice Dance Social Sessions.

Division 3 - Teams should consist of mid-level players. In example: played in the ACHA (Div 2 or Div 3). In addition, these teams could consist of former high-level youth players (AA or A). 18-Game regular season.

Division 3 - Teams should consist of mid-level players. In example: played in the ACHA (Div 2 or Div 3). In addition, these teams could consist of former high-level youth players (AA or A). 18-Game regular season.

Division 1 - Teams should consist of high-level players. In example: played Junior hockey (BCHL, USHL, NAHL), ACHA (Div 1/Div 2), NCAA (Div I/III), or pro-level hockey. In addition, these teams could consist of former high-level youth players (AAA). 18-Game regular season.
